evidence_samples.lxx_text_start: ὁ λαὸς ὁ πορευόμενος ἐν σκότει ἴδετε φῶς μέγα οἱ κατοικοῦντες ἐν χώρᾳ καὶ σκιᾷ θανάτου φῶς λάμψει ἐφ’ ὑμᾶς
evidence_samples.kjv_text_start: The people that walked in darkness have seen a great light: they that dwell in the land of the shadow of death, upon them hath the light shined.

evidence_samples.lxx_text_start: φάγεται γὰρ Μανασση τοῦ Εφραιμ καὶ Εφραιμ τοῦ Μανασση ὅτι ἅμα πολιορκήσουσιν τὸν Ιουδαν ἐπὶ τούτοις πᾶσιν οὐκ ἀπεστράφη ὁ θυμός ἀλλ’ ἔτι ἡ χεὶρ ὑψηλή
evidence_samples.kjv_text_start: Manasseh, Ephraim; and Ephraim, Manasseh: [and] they together [shall be] against Judah. For all this his anger is not turned away, but his hand [is] stretched out still.

groups.notes: Corrected after full boundary review. CrossWire source Isa.8.23 aligns with KJV 9.1, then source Isa.9.1-20 aligns continuously with KJV 9.2-21. Target 9.13 is represented by source 9.12.

evidence_samples.lxx_text_start: ὡς κηρὸς ἀπὸ πυρὸς τήκεται καὶ κατακαύσει πῦρ τοὺς ὑπεναντίους καὶ φανερὸν ἔσται τὸ ὄνομα κυρίου ἐν τοῖς ὑπεναντίοις ἀπὸ προσώπου σου ἔθνη ταραχθήσονται
evidence_samples.kjv_text_start: As [when] the melting fire burneth, the fire causeth the waters to boil, to make thy name known to thine adversaries, [that] the nations may tremble at thy presence!

evidence_samples.lxx_text_start: καὶ ἐπὶ πᾶσι τούτοις ἀνέσχου κύριε καὶ ἐσιώπησας καὶ ἐταπείνωσας ἡμᾶς σφόδρα
evidence_samples.kjv_text_start: Wilt thou refrain thyself for these [things], O LORD? wilt thou hold thy peace, and afflict us very sore?

groups.notes: GreekDoc labels target Isa.64.1 as [LM 63:19b]. CrossWire source Isa.63.19 contains the segment after its ordinary 63.19 material; source 64.1-11 safely maps to target 64.2-12. Import needs token-span split for target 64.1.

evidence_samples.lxx_text_start: διὰ τοῦτο ἐπ’ αὐτῷ ἐσπούδακα νουθετούμενος δὲ ἐφρόντισα αὐτοῦ ἐπὶ τούτῳ ἀπὸ προσώπου αὐτοῦ κατασπουδασθῶ κατανοήσω καὶ πτοηθήσομαι ἐξ αὐτοῦ
evidence_samples.kjv_text_start: Therefore am I troubled at his presence: when I consider, I am afraid of him.

evidence_samples.lxx_text_start: οὐ γὰρ ᾔδειν ὅτι ἐπελεύσεταί μοι σκότος πρὸ προσώπου δέ μου ἐκάλυψεν γνόφος
evidence_samples.kjv_text_start: Because I was not cut off before the darkness, [neither] hath he covered the darkness from my face.

groups.notes: GreekDoc marks target Job.23.14 N/A. CrossWire compact source Job.23.14 aligns with KJV Job.23.15 and source 23.14-16 safely maps to target 23.15-17.

evidence_samples.lxx_text_start: ἄξεις δὲ δράκοντα ἐν ἀγκίστρῳ περιθήσεις δὲ φορβεὰν περὶ ῥῖνα αὐτοῦ
evidence_samples.kjv_text_start: Canst thou draw out leviathan with an hook? or his tongue with a cord [which] thou lettest down?

evidence_samples.lxx_text_start: ἐπιθήσεις δὲ αὐτῷ χεῖρα μνησθεὶς πόλεμον τὸν γινόμενον ἐν σώματι αὐτοῦ καὶ μηκέτι γινέσθω
evidence_samples.kjv_text_start: Lay thine hand upon him, remember the battle, do no more.

groups.notes: GreekDoc chapter 41 explicitly annotates target 41.1-8 with [LM 40.25] through [LM 40.32], confirming the CrossWire source chapter-boundary mapping.

evidence_samples.lxx_text_start: οὐχ ἑόρακας αὐτὸν οὐδὲ ἐπὶ τοῖς λεγομένοις τεθαύμακας
evidence_samples.kjv_text_start: Behold, the hope of him is in vain: shall not [one] be cast down even at the sight of him?

evidence_samples.lxx_text_start: πᾶν ὑψηλὸν ὁρᾷ αὐτὸς δὲ βασιλεὺς πάντων τῶν ἐν τοῖς ὕδασιν
evidence_samples.kjv_text_start: He beholdeth all high [things]: he [is] a king over all the children of pride.

groups.notes: After the eight LM 40.25-32 rows, CrossWire source Job.41.1-26 aligns continuously with KJV Job.41.9-34; beginning and ending texts were checked.

evidence_samples.lxx_text_start: καὶ εἶδον καὶ ἰδοὺ τροχὸς εἷς ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς ἐχόμενος τῶν ζῴων τοῖς τέσσαρσιν
evidence_samples.kjv_text_start: Now as I beheld the living creatures, behold one wheel upon the earth by the living creatures, with his four faces.

evidence_samples.lxx_text_start: ὡς ὅρασις τόξου ὅταν ᾖ ἐν τῇ νεφέλῃ ἐν ἡμέρᾳ ὑετοῦ οὕτως ἡ στάσις τοῦ φέγγους κυκλόθεν αὕτη ἡ ὅρασις ὁμοιώματος δόξης κυρίου καὶ εἶδον καὶ πίπτω ἐπὶ πρόσωπόν μου καὶ ἤκουσα φωνὴν λ
evidence_samples.kjv_text_start: As the appearance of the bow that is in the cloud in the day of rain, so [was] the appearance of the brightness round about. This [was] the appearance of the likeness of the glory

groups.notes: GreekDoc marks target Ezek.1.14 N/A in both displayed Greek columns; CrossWire source 1.14-27 aligns with target 1.15-28.

evidence_samples.lxx_text_start: καὶ ἦραν τὰ χερουβιν τοῦτο τὸ ζῷον ὃ εἶδον ἐπὶ τοῦ ποταμοῦ τοῦ Χοβαρ
evidence_samples.kjv_text_start: And the cherubims were lifted up. This [is] the living creature that I saw by the river of Chebar.

evidence_samples.lxx_text_start: καὶ ὁμοίωσις τῶν προσώπων αὐτῶν ταῦτα τὰ πρόσωπά ἐστιν ἃ εἶδον ὑποκάτω τῆς δόξης θεοῦ Ισραηλ ἐπὶ τοῦ ποταμοῦ τοῦ Χοβαρ καὶ αὐτὰ ἕκαστον κατὰ πρόσωπον αὐτῶν ἐπορεύοντο
evidence_samples.kjv_text_start: And the likeness of their faces [was] the same faces which I saw by the river of Chebar, their appearances and themselves: they went every one straight forward.

groups.notes: CrossWire omits target Ezek.10.14 and source 10.14-21 aligns with target 10.15-22. GreekDoc is witness-sensitive: one Greek column marks 10.14 N/A while the other preserves bracketed text.

evidence_samples.lxx_text_start: καὶ ἐγένετο λόγος κυρίου πρός με λέγων
evidence_samples.kjv_text_start: Moreover the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

evidence_samples.lxx_text_start: καὶ εἶπα μηδαμῶς κύριε κύριε αὐτοὶ λέγουσιν πρός με οὐχὶ παραβολή ἐστιν λεγομένη αὕτη
evidence_samples.kjv_text_start: Then said I, Ah Lord GOD! they say of me, Doth he not speak parables?

groups.notes: GreekDoc marks targets Neh.11.20-21 N/A. CrossWire compact source 11.19 resumes with the Uzzi overseer line corresponding to KJV 11.22 and continues through the Beer-sheba line at target 11.27. Some list rows are shorter than MT/KJV.

evidence_samples.lxx_text_start: καὶ ἐπαύλεις αὐτῶν Λαχις καὶ ἀγροὶ αὐτῆς καὶ παρενεβάλοσαν ἐν Βεηρσαβεε
evidence_samples.kjv_text_start: Zanoah, Adullam, and [in] their villages, at Lachish, and the fields thereof, at Azekah, and [in] the villages thereof. And they dwelt from Beer-sheba unto the valley of Hinnom.

groups.notes: GreekDoc marks targets Neh.11.28-29 N/A. CrossWire compact source 11.25 is the Lachish and Beer-sheba row corresponding to the surviving shorter Greek form of KJV 11.30.

evidence_samples.lxx_text_start: καὶ οἱ υἱοὶ Βενιαμιν ἀπὸ Γαβα Μαχαμας
evidence_samples.kjv_text_start: The children also of Benjamin from Geba [dwelt] at Michmash, and Aija, and Beth-el, and [in] their villages,

groups.notes: CrossWire compact source 11.26 is the shorter Benjamin/Geba/Michmash row corresponding to KJV 11.31.

evidence_samples.lxx_text_start: καὶ ἀπὸ τῶν Λευιτῶν μερίδες Ιουδα τῷ Βενιαμιν
evidence_samples.kjv_text_start: And of the Levites [were] divisions [in] Judah, [and] in Benjamin.

groups.notes: GreekDoc marks targets Neh.11.32-35 N/A. CrossWire compact source 11.27 is the Levite divisions line corresponding to KJV 11.36.

evidence_samples.lxx_text_start: οὗτοι ἄρχοντες τῶν ἱερέων καὶ ἀδελφοὶ αὐτῶν ἐν ἡμέραις Ἰησοῦ
evidence_samples.kjv_text_start: Sallu, Amok, Hilkiah, Jedaiah. These [were] the chief of the priests and of their brethren in the days of Jeshua.

evidence_samples.lxx_text_start: καὶ πᾶς Ισραηλ ἐν ἡμέραις Ζοροβαβελ διδόντες μερίδας τῶν ᾀδόντων καὶ τῶν πυλωρῶν λόγον ἡμέρας ἐν ἡμέρᾳ αὐτοῦ καὶ ἁγιάζοντες τοῖς Λευίταις καὶ οἱ Λευῖται ἁγιάζοντες τοῖς υἱοῖς Ααρων
evidence_samples.kjv_text_start: And all Israel in the days of Zerubbabel, and in the days of Nehemiah, gave the portions of the singers and the porters, every day his portion: and they sanctified [holy things] unto the Levites; and the Levites sanctified [them] unto the c

groups.notes: GreekDoc marks targets Neh.12.4-6 N/A. CrossWire compact source 12.4 is the chief-priests conclusion corresponding to KJV 12.7; the +3 shift remains stable through source 12.44 -> target 12.47.

evidence_samples.lxx_text_start: καὶ οὐκ ἀπορηθήσεται ὁ ἐν στενοχωρίᾳ ὢν ἕως καιροῦ τοῦτο πρῶτον ποίει ταχὺ ποίει χώρα Ζαβουλων ἡ γῆ Νεφθαλιμ ὁδὸν θαλάσσης καὶ οἱ λοιποὶ οἱ τὴν παραλίαν κατοικοῦντες καὶ πέραν τοῦ Ιορδάνου Γαλιλαία τῶν ἐθνῶν τὰ μέρη τῆς Ιουδαίας
evidence_samples.kjv_text_start: Nevertheless the dimness [shall] not [be] such as [was] in her vexation, when at the first he lightly afflicted the land of Zebulun and the land of Naphtali, and afterward did more grievously afflict [her by] the way of the sea, beyond Jord

metadata.purpose: Manual token-span/merge LXX->KJV projection rules that require textual-policy decisions rather than automatic exact-pass selection.
metadata.source: CrossWire LXX source_cache/crosswire_lxx.zip; Daniel rules use owner-approved Theodotion x-2 as the primary displayed Daniel recension.
info_source_summary_notes: Alternative ancient Greek source supplementation: Ps.116.17 suffix καὶ ἐν ὀνόματι κυρίου ἐπικαλέσομαι is attested by Theodoret of Cyrus, Interpretatio in Psalmos, PG 80.1805 (public-domain scan: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Patrologia_Graeca_Vol._080.pdf); CrossWire LXX supplies the prefix.

Owner-approved alternative-source supplementation from H. B. Swete, The Old Testament in Greek according to the Septuagint (public-domain scan: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_Old_Testament_in_Greek,_according_to_the_Septuagint_(IA_cu31924012092437).pdf). Selected readings are stored as literal Greek for witness-sensitive refs Josh.20.4-6; 1Sam.23.12; 1Kgs.6.18; 1Kgs.14.7-9,14-16,19-20; Prov.8.33; Prov.23.23; Lam.3.22-24; Ezek.10.14, and CrossWire-specific omission refs Gen.35.21; Exod.25.6; 1Chr.1.11-23; 1Chr.16.24; 1Kgs.6.11-14; 1Kgs.15.32; Neh.3.7; Ps.116.14. GreekDoc polyglot pages were used only as alignment controls. Strong numbers are omitted for these literal rows pending reliable manual lexical mapping rather than guessed.
